name: Norte de Santander (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the blood spilled by the patriots in the wars of independence
the suffering and mourning of the people throughout the struggle
the gold and the wealth of the department's soil

description: A field divided horizontally with red over black, charged with four yellow five-pointed stars arranged across the field.
history: Norte de Santander Department was created in 1910 when the old Department of Santander was split. Its red and black flag with yellow stars draws on the colours of independence-era Cucuta, the department's capital and the site of the 1821 Congress of Cucuta.

features.symbolism: four yellow stars distributed across both halves of the flag
